milestones.title: Skeleton — both services stood up, stack wiring, CI/CD publish, list + add + POST + sign-in + nav
milestones.goal: The address-book system-of-record (renamed in place from trade-imports-operators) and the net-new ins-frontend front-door both boot from published images in the workspace stack; a signed-in user reaches the org-scoped address book, sees the list (empty-state + pagination), adds an address, and it persists. Establishes the Standard Address Block, the camelCase wire, the org-path + cv-040 authorisation trust boundary, and the untyped model.

milestones.title: View / edit / delete — by-id GET, full-replace PUT, soft-DELETE + the deleted flag
milestones.goal: Complete the CRUD surface: an address details page with Edit and Delete, a full-replace edit (omitted optionals cleared), a two-step delete confirmation performing a soft delete, and the by-id endpoint that returns a soft-deleted row flagged so notifications can resolve old references. AC8 (edits reach drafts, never submitted) is explicitly NOT closed here — it is a follow-on gated behind EUDPA-295 (see m4-05).

milestones.title: Server-side text search over the address book
milestones.goal: Extend the list endpoint with ?q= — a case-insensitive, partial-word regex $or over name/townOrCity/postcode (NOT $text), org-scoped in the DB query, excluding soft-deleted, preserving pagination; a typed country name is resolved by the FE to its alpha-2 code and passed as an optional countryCode term the query ORs over the stored countryCode field (cv-048 FE-resolve). The list page gains a search input with a no-results state distinct from the org-empty state.

milestones.title: Reference + resolve-on-read — journey select pages, hydrating resolve, EUDPA-198 model trim
milestones.goal: The journey stops copying party details and instead stores an addressId reference resolved to current details on read (no fan-out into drafts). Select pages show the org's real address book (four referenceable roles only), the notification model drops addressLine3 (absorbing EUDPA-198), and the operator->address/consignment-party terminology is retired journey-side. Introduces the amend-start capture bug that EUDPA-295 fixes.
milestones.sequencingNote: The spec blockingGraph makes EUDPA-186 (M2) and EUDPA-294 (M3) independent siblings (both blockedBy only 287+286; neither blocks the other). m3-01/m3-02 nonetheless depend on m2-02 by INTENTIONAL milestone sequencing — M2 closes on the m2-02 milestone walk-through gate, and M3 begins after it so the running-stack address-book (CRUD + search) is proven before the journey is repointed at it. This is a build-loop sequencing choice, not an authority-graph edge; if parallel M2/M3 delivery is later wanted, the m2-02 edges on m3-01/m3-02 may be dropped without violating the blockingGraph.

milestones.title: Deleted-address submit gate + freeze-on-submit (validate-then-freeze) + EUDPA-286.AC8
milestones.goal: Block submitting a notification that references a soft-deleted address (FE review-page UX + BE authoritative reject), then freeze the resolved party details into a snapshot at submit (reusing NotificationContentSnapshot), retaining the addressId. The two mutate the same submit transaction: EUDPA-295 lands second and inserts the snapshot capture AFTER the deleted-gate (validate-then-freeze), pinned by an integration test. Closes EUDPA-286.AC8 (edits reach drafts, never submitted). Hard gate before go-live.
milestones.coordination: EUDPA-293 and EUDPA-295 have no hard build-order in the blockingGraph but share the submit transaction; 293 is built first, 295 second, and the second (295) owns the validate-then-freeze ordering + integration test (m4-04).

increments.title: Rename trade-imports-operators to trade-imports-address-book in place
increments.description: Mechanical rename-in-place keeping git history (cv-003). Java package uk.gov.defra.trade.imports.operators -> ...addressbook; operator sub-package -> address; class Operator -> Address; @Document(collection="operators") (Operator.java:30) -> "addresses"; mongo db name -> trade-imports-address-book (cv-042). Keep port 8089 and the Trade-Imports-Organisation-Id header name (cv-042). One rename commit BEFORE any behaviour change so the real-work diffs stay readable. No field/model changes yet.
increments.salvageVsRebuild: salvage-modified (rename only) — repos/trade-imports-operators whole repo on feat/EUDPA-58-address-book
tddTargets: Existing Testcontainers IT suite (IntegrationBase Mongo 7.0) stays green after the rename — the rename must be behaviour-preserving
Build/context loads under the new package name

increments.title: Flip Jackson naming strategy snake_case -> camelCase
increments.description: The single load-bearing wire change (cv-001). Flip PropertyNamingStrategies.SNAKE_CASE (JacksonConfig.java:29) -> LOWER_CAMEL_CASE, matching reference-data (Country.java:15-16, no override). Keep failOnUnknownProperties(false). Delete the @JsonProperty("address_line_1"/"_2") overrides (OperatorRequest.java:31/35, OperatorResponse.java:24-25). Simplify GlobalExceptionHandler.wireFieldName (:198-210) + the SNAKE_CASE constant (:49-50) + the IdentityHeaderFilter problemWriter (:64-67) to the identity field-name path — under camelCase the wire name IS the Java field name. Problem traceId becomes camelCase (was snake trace_id).
tddTargets: IT asserts a create/get response serialises camelCase keys (addressLine1 not address_line_1, countryCode, townOrCity)
Problem+json IT asserts traceId (camelCase) present
Validation-error IT asserts the errors map is keyed by camelCase field names

increments.title: Reshape the Address document + indexes — untyped, countryCode, org-keyed
increments.description: Rebuild the model to entities.address. town->townOrCity, telephone->phone (Operator.java:54,62). country display-name -> countryCode ISO alpha-2 (Operator.java:60 + Javadoc :16-20 — reverses c-004/D1, cv-011). DELETE operatorType (:46, cv-017), approvalNumber + transporterCategory (:66-68, cv-013), crn (:70, demoted cv-010). Promote organisationId (:72) to primary scope key. DELETE the crn_status_type_created compound index (:31-33); promote+extend org_status (:34) to {organisationId:1, status:1, createdAt:-1} — the single index behind every org-scoped, status-bounded, newest-first read. Keep the ACTIVE/DELETED tombstone enum (rename OperatorStatus->AddressStatus); expose a derived deleted boolean on the wire (cv-016). Keep Lombok @Data/@Builder shape.
tddTargets: Unit: Address builds with the reduced field set; no operatorType/approvalNumber/transporterCategory/crn accessors
IT: the {organisationId,status,createdAt} index is created and used (explain/IndexIT re-keyed org-for-crn)
IT: countryCode round-trips as given (e.g. 'FR'), not converted to a display name

increments.title: Org-path controller + cv-040 path-vs-session authorisation
increments.description: Rebase @RequestMapping("/operators") -> "/organisation/{orgId}/addresses" (OperatorController.java:37); every handler gains @PathVariable String orgId. IdentityHeaderFilter (salvage-modified :50-114): keep the fail-fast 400 problem+json shape; require Trade-Imports-Organisation-Id on every /organisation/** request; DELETE the entire crn branch (:52,:80-84,:93) and the POST-only org-id special case (:86-90); path prefix (:57)->/organisation; stash organisationId in MDC. NEW authorisation compare: a single explicit guard authoriseOrg(orgId, sessionOrg) at the top of every handler — if the forwarded Trade-Imports-Organisation-Id header != path orgId, throw NotFoundException -> 404 (not 403, no existence disclosure, cv-040). After the guard, scope every repository call by orgId. Delete the operator_type param (:86) and operatorType log/args (:89-90,:122).
increments.salvageVsRebuild: salvage-modified + new authorise step — OperatorController.java, IdentityHeaderFilter.java:50-114
tddTargets: IT (AddressScopingIT, load-bearing rewrite): org B cannot read org A's address — byte-identical 404 for cross-org id vs unknown id
IT (cv-040 new): a caller whose Trade-Imports-Organisation-Id header disagrees with the path orgId gets 404
IT: a missing/blank org header is a platform auth rejection, not a modelled business 400

increments.title: create-address (POST) + mixed validation
increments.description: POST /organisation/{orgId}/addresses -> 201 + server-assigned id (287.AC13). Port OperatorRequest->AddressRequest keeping @NotBlank/@Size for the V4 table (name 255, addressLine1 255, addressLine2 opt 255, townOrCity 100, county opt 100, postcode 12, phone 20, email 254). countryCode: @NotBlank only, NO @Size(255)/list validation (stored-as-given, cv-011/b-countrycode-store); @NotBlank enforces non-blank presence (matches the contract minLength:1, a presence not a format check). phone: not format-validated. email: KEEP @Email (cv-044). DELETE operatorType/approvalNumber/transporterCategory components, @ValidTransporterFields + both validator files, and the operatorType-immutability reject. ADD: reject a stray type/role field with a per-field 400 (cv-044) via explicit @Null-constrained type/role components — a supplied value lands in the errors map keyed type/role while OTHER unknown/server-assigned fields stay silently ignored (Zalando failOnUnknownProperties(false); NOT additionalProperties:false / failOnUnknownProperties(true), which would 400 a benign echoed readOnly field). Routes through the existing MethodArgumentNotValidException handler. id/orgId/deleted/createdAt/modifiedAt are server-assigned, never from the body; new rows deleted=false.
tddTargets: IT: valid POST -> 201, Location header, echoes camelCase Address with a new id, deleted=false
IT: missing mandatory + over-max -> 400 problem+json with a per-field errors map naming each field
IT: malformed email -> 400 (email in errors map)
IT: a stray type/role field -> 400 keyed by that field name (cv-044); an unrelated unknown field (or an echoed readOnly id/createdAt) is IGNORED not rejected
IT: countryCode 'FR' accepted as-given (no list validation); blank countryCode -> 400; free-string phone accepted

increments.title: list-addresses (GET collection) + config page-size + exclude soft-deleted
increments.description: GET /organisation/{orgId}/addresses -> top-level object {items,page,pageSize,totalItems,totalPages} (camelCase, never a bare array), newest-first, EXCLUDING soft-deleted (status=ACTIVE literal in the query, b-soft-delete). Org isolation is in the DB query not a post-filter (186.AC5, b-org-owned). Re-rule page size to a server-side config: inject @Value("${address-book.list.page-size:25}") (mirrors animals-backend notification.list.page-size + PageRequest.of); drop the pageSize method arg + the page_size request param + MAX_PAGE_SIZE range guard (OperatorService.java:33,79-86). Keep page as the only pagination param (1-based; page<1 -> BadRequestException). ?q= search is NOT in this increment (M2).
tddTargets: IT: list returns page 1 of 25 (config), sorted newest-first, camelCase envelope keys
IT: soft-deleted rows are excluded from the list
IT: a second org's rows never appear (org-scoped query)
IT: page<1 -> 400; no page_size/pageSize request param is honoured

increments.title: Bootstrap net-new ins-frontend from the CDP node template + agent rails
increments.description: Net-new repo (cv-003) from the CDP Node.js frontend template — Hapi + nunjucks + govuk-frontend, GOV.UK header/footer/layout/start page, redis session, /health. Phase 0 is the CDP scaffold + .claude/ + CLAUDE.md rails (workspace convention: rails land first), not features. Config: salvage the CDP config shape; add the tradeImportsAddressBookApi block (TRADE_IMPORTS_ADDRESS_BOOK_URL, default port 8089, cv-042). No address-book UI yet.
increments.salvageVsRebuild: new — CDP node frontend template; config shape salvaged from animals-frontend config.js
tddTargets: App boots, /health 200 (CDP smoke)
Config loads TRADE_IMPORTS_ADDRESS_BOOK_URL with the 8089 default

increments.title: Sign-in through the Defra ID stub + org-claim mapping
increments.description: MIRROR (not move) the Defra ID bell strategy from animals-frontend src/plugins/auth.js:40-51 into INS: profile.organisationId = payload.currentRelationshipId (:49), spread onto credentials (auth/controller.js:46-53). Salvage src/server/auth/controller.js signin/signinOidc/signout/signoutOidc verbatim. Add the defraId config block (mirror config.js:175-244) with its OWN DEFRA_ID_* env + DEFRA_ID_REDIRECT_URL on port 3002. Stub OIDC discovery at :3007; keep the single-origin rule (host.docker.internal end-to-end). D23: a signed-in user always has an org — build NO null-org handling. Reuses EUDPA-59 (Defra ID stub), the config/reuse prerequisite.
tddTargets: E2E: sign in via the Defra ID stub reaches a signed-in page; sign-out returns to the start page
Unit/component: request.auth.credentials.organisationId is populated from currentRelationshipId

increments.title: INS shell navigation — two links (Dashboard + Address book)
increments.description: Salvage build-navigation.js:4-20 (currently Dashboard/Address book/About). DROP the About entry (:15-19) so the shell shows exactly two links: Dashboard (target out of scope, future unified dashboard) and Address book (current flag via path.startsWith('/address-book'), already at :13). Selecting Address book opens /address-book. Absorbs EUDPA-185.AC1/AC2 (cv-028).
tddTargets: Component: nav renders exactly two links; About absent
Component: the Address book link carries current when path starts /address-book

increments.title: address-book-client — rewrite to camelCase + org-path + forward org header
increments.description: Salvage the operators-client.js:111-156 STRUCTURE (fetch + throwOnError + mapApiErrorsToFormErrors) but rebuild the mapping. Rename -> address-book-client.js. DELETE FIELD_MAP/wireToForm/toApiOperator/fromApiOperator (:10-71) — camelCase wire, body/response pass through (cv-001). DELETE the journey-side toNotificationOperator/toTransporter (:80-109 — those belong to animals-frontend select pages). Paths /operators... -> /organisation/{orgId}/addresses... (:167,201,226,254,281); orgId = session organisationId as a path arg on every method. DROP the operator_type query param (:172-174, cv-017). Forward Trade-Imports-Organisation-Id on EVERY call (today only createOperator sends it, :232) — cv-040. Method renames list/get/create/update/delete-Address. Keep throwOnError + mapApiErrorsToFormErrors (mapping becomes identity on the key). This increment wires the list + create methods against m0-05/m0-06; get/put/delete methods are added in M1 alongside their pages.
tddTargets: Unit (nock at the HTTP boundary): listAddresses(orgId) GETs /organisation/{orgId}/addresses with the org header, parses the camelCase {items,page,pageSize,totalItems,totalPages} envelope
Unit (nock): createAddress(orgId, body) POSTs camelCase body, forwards the org header, returns the created id
Unit: a 400 problem+json maps to form errors keyed by camelCase field names

increments.title: Address Joi schema (untyped) + country picker + single-source parity test
increments.description: Salvage buildOperatorSchema (operator-schema.js:25-113) -> buildAddressSchema. DELETE the operatorType field (:33-40) and both transporter-conditional branches approvalNumber/transporterCategory (:91-110) — cv-013/cv-017. Rename city->townOrCity, telephone->phone; max-lengths/mandatory flags unchanged (the V4 table). country->countryCode: Joi.valid(...mdmCodes) over alpha-2 CODES, keep required; email keeps format validation, phone stays format-unvalidated (cv-044). Salvage getOperatorFormCountries (operator-countries.js:15-28) -> getAddressFormCountries: GB-prepend over reference-data /countries, THROW on empty list (never degrade); option value binds code not name (cv-011); strip the stale c-004 display-name Javadoc (:6-12). ADD the b-single-source-validation parity/convention test asserting identical field maxLengths + mandatory flags across this Joi schema and the Java Bean Validation (no runtime-shared artifact) — including countryCode mandatory (Joi required <-> Java @NotBlank <-> contract minLength:1).
tddTargets: Unit: schema rejects missing mandatory / over-max per the V4 table; accepts a valid block; no operatorType/transporter fields exist
Unit: countryCode validates against MDM alpha-2 codes (mandatory, non-blank); email format enforced; phone free-string
Unit: getAddressFormCountries GB-prepends and throws on an empty MDM list
Parity test: Joi maxLengths + mandatory flags match the Java Bean Validation rules (countryCode mandatory on both sides)

increments.title: Add-address page — single page (type page removed), POST create, cancel, success banner
increments.description: DELETE the whole type-selection page src/server/address-book/add/type/ (controller.js:34-64) and the operator-types catalogue (untyped, cv-017). Collapse to a single add page: GET /address-book/add renders the details form directly; POST validates (buildAddressSchema) + createAddress. Remove the /add/details sub-route and the operator_type query-param guard (add/details/controller.js:53-57). Drop operatorType/isTransporter from the view model. buildCountryItems value:name->value:code (cv-011). Cancel discards input, navigates back to the list, no POST (287.AC12, b-cancel-add — no confirm guard, GDS silent discard). On save, return to /address-book with a one-shot success banner (cv-021/b-success-banner); banner copy 'X added to your address book'. Error summary + inline errors on 400 (287.AC9).
tddTargets: Component: GET /address-book/add renders the details form with no preceding type page
E2E: fill + Save -> redirect to /address-book, success banner shown once, address in the list
E2E: invalid submit -> error summary + inline errors, no create
E2E: Cancel -> back to list, no address created

increments.title: Address book list page — table, empty-state, pagination, success banner
increments.description: Salvage list/controller.js + list/index.njk. Untyped strip: remove the Type column (index.njk:66,78) + mapOperatorRow.type (list/controller.js:73); remove the operator-type filter govukSelect (:42-50) + buildOperatorTypeOptions + the operator_type param throughout the query-string builder. Columns Name / Address / Country: the single Address column composes addressLine1, townOrCity, postcode joined by commas (oi-address-column min set; buildAddressLine at controller.js:57-67 narrowed); Country renders countryCode. Empty-state (b-empty-state, 287.AC7): totalItems===0 AND no q -> 'no addresses yet' + add action. Pagination (cv-025/b-config-page-size): salvage buildPaginationLinks with the buildQueryString arg (notification-helper.js:141-191) -> numbered govukPagination; render pageSize/totalPages as handed, never hard-code 25 (NOT server/home/_pagination.njk). One-shot success banner via getSessionValue clear-on-read (controller.js:108). Copy rename operator->address (cv-015). Search UI is NOT in this increment (M2).
tddTargets: E2E: signed-in list shows the org's addresses in Name/Address/Country columns, no Type column
E2E: an org with no addresses shows the empty-state + add action
E2E: >25 addresses paginates with numbered govukPagination; FE does not send a page size
Component: copy says 'address'/'Add a new address', never 'operator'

increments.title: Per-repo CI/CD image publishing (the AC1 prerequisite)
increments.description: ji-cicd-publish — the prerequisite that makes the workspace PR's image: references and 'boot from published images' meetable and gives branch-tag parity (rule 2) an image to pick up. address-book: keep publish.yml/publish-hotfix.yml (cdp-build-action at publish.yml:31 derives the image from the repo name, so the GitHub repo rename operators->address-book renames :latest/release automatically) and ADD a publish-branch.yml (thin caller of the reusable workflow, image-name: trade-imports-address-book) — operators lacks one, reference-data has the template. ins-frontend (net-new, no pipeline): add publish.yml + publish-branch.yml (image-name: trade-imports-ins-frontend) + check-pull-request.yml, copied from animals-frontend. Ordering: CI publishes FIRST, then the workspace PR sets image: (chicken-and-egg).
increments.salvageVsRebuild: new (branch-publish for address-book) + new pipeline (ins-frontend) — reference-data publish-branch.yml:15,17; animals-frontend .github/workflows/*
tddTargets: CI green: address-book publishes :latest + a branch-tagged image under the new name
CI green: ins-frontend publishes :latest + a branch-tagged image
Branch-parity: a feat/EUDPA-58-address-book image tag exists for both so the stack --branch flag can pick it up

increments.title: Workspace wiring PR — declare both services + rename operators wiring to address-book
increments.description: ji-workspace-wiring — the separate AC1 workspace PR adding BOTH services (unmeetable until m0-14 publishes). backend.compose.yml: new address-book stanza modelled on the ref-data stanza (:73-106) — profiles[backend], depends_on mongodb (no floci-init), PORT 8089, MONGO_DATABASE trade-imports-address-book, health curl :8089/health, image defradigital/trade-imports-address-book, ports 8089:8089; flip animals-backend TRADE_IMPORTS_OPERATORS_URL (:20) -> TRADE_IMPORTS_ADDRESS_BOOK_URL. frontend.compose.yml: new ins-frontend stanza (profiles[frontend], depends_on redis, PORT 3002, its OWN DEFRA_ID_* block, TRADE_IMPORTS_ADDRESS_BOOK_URL, wget health, image, 3002:3002); flip animals-frontend TRADE_IMPORTS_OPERATORS_URL (:49). dev.compose.yml: Java address-book overlay (target dev-run, src mount, mirrors ref-data :54-60) + Node ins-frontend overlay (target development, src mount, mirrors animals-frontend :2-8). Makefile: REPOS +2, JAVA_REPOS +1 (address-book), NODE_REPOS +1 (ins-frontend). CLAUDE.md: two repo-map rows. Tests repo (same branch name): rename seeds/mongodb/30-operators-seed.js -> 30-address-book-seed.js, retarget db, drop operatorType from seed rows. NEVER edit docker/stack/.staged/. The two already-dirty compose edits become the address-book-URL edits inside this PR.
increments.salvageVsRebuild: new — backend.compose.yml:20,73-106; frontend.compose.yml:49; dev.compose.yml:2-8,54-60; Makefile:2,4,5; CLAUDE.md:55-56; tests 30-operators-seed.js
tddTargets: make docker-compose (published images) boots both services; both /health pass
make docker-compose-dev hot-reloads both (Spring DevTools for the Java service, node dev overlay for ins-frontend)
Full-stack E2E (walk-through): sign in on ins-frontend :3002 -> add an address -> it appears in the org list; seeded org data resolves from the trade-imports-address-book db

increments.title: get-address (by-id GET) — includes soft-deleted, flagged
increments.description: GET /organisation/{orgId}/addresses/{id} returns a single address INCLUDING a soft-deleted one flagged deleted:true — the ONLY endpoint that surfaces a deleted row (286.AC7; 294.AC2 asymmetry: list hides deleted, by-id returns it). Ports OperatorService get-includes-tombstone (OperatorService.java:139-141) via findByIdAndOrganisationId (repository re-key findByIdAndCrn -> findByIdAndOrganisationId, :23). 404 for an unknown id or a cross-org id (cv-040, indistinguishable — no existence disclosure). This is the resolve-on-read source for 294 and the deleted signal for 293.
tddTargets: IT: GET a live address -> 200 with deleted:false
IT: GET a soft-deleted address -> 200 with deleted:true (only this endpoint surfaces it)
IT: unknown id -> 404; cross-org id -> byte-identical 404

increments.title: update-address (PUT) — full replace, clears omitted optionals
increments.description: PUT /organisation/{orgId}/addresses/{id} FULL REPLACE — every field from the body; an omitted optional (addressLine2, county) is CLEARED not left unchanged (286.AC7, PUT not PATCH). Port OperatorService.update (:164-191) MINUS the operatorType-immutability reject (:171-174) — nothing is immutable now. findByIdAndOrganisationId, filter DELETED tombstone -> 404, set every mutable field (null for an absent optional writes through), save bumps modifiedAt. Same single-source validation + stray-type/role rejection as create (a round-tripped GET body that echoes readOnly id/createdAt/modifiedAt/deleted PUTs cleanly — those are ignored, not rejected). 404 for unknown/cross-org (cv-040). This increment delivers the per-field edit validation (286.AC4) on the update surface.
tddTargets: IT: PUT replaces all fields; a body omitting addressLine2/county CLEARS them
IT: PUT with invalid fields -> 400 per-field errors map (286.AC4)
IT: PUT unknown/cross-org id -> 404; PUT a soft-deleted tombstone -> 404
IT: modifiedAt is bumped, createdAt unchanged

increments.title: delete-address (DELETE) — soft-delete, 204, idempotent
increments.description: DELETE /organisation/{orgId}/addresses/{id} sets deleted (status->DELETED), returns 204 WITHOUT removing the record. Org-wide: disappears for the whole org from list/search + notification-creation selection thereafter, but stays resolvable by-id (m1-01). Ports OperatorService soft-delete (:209-222): idempotent (already-DELETED -> 204, no second modifiedAt bump). 404 for unknown/cross-org id (cv-040).
tddTargets: IT: DELETE a live address -> 204, row kept, status DELETED
IT: the deleted address is excluded from list/search but returned flagged by GET-by-id
IT: DELETE an already-deleted address -> 204, idempotent, no state change
IT: DELETE unknown/cross-org id -> 404

increments.title: Address view page — details + Edit/Delete actions
increments.description: Salvage view/controller.js + view/index.njk. Add the getAddress method to address-book-client (GET by-id, forward org header). buildRows renders Name / Address / Country(countryCode) / Telephone(phone) / Email as a govukSummaryList (view/controller.js:44-52). Remove the transporter rows block + TRANSPORTER_CATEGORY_LABELS (:12-15,54-66) and the Type row (:47) — untyped. Edit -> /address-book/{id}/edit, Delete -> /address-book/{id}/delete (:101-102). Routes already key on an id (index.js:40/45/55) — rename the param operatorId->id, ports directly (no slug to reconcile). Copy operator->address (cv-015).
tddTargets: E2E: view page shows name + full address + contact details read-only, with Edit and Delete
Component: no Type row, no transporter rows
Unit (nock): getAddress(orgId,id) GETs the by-id path with the org header

increments.title: Edit address page — prefilled, full-replace PUT, cancel, success banner
increments.description: Salvage edit/controller.js + edit/index.njk. Add the updateAddress method (PUT, forward org header). Same field set as add, prefilled from getAddress (:73-85). Drop operatorType/isTransporter from the view model (:52-53,90,103); field renames (city->townOrCity, telephone->phone, country->countryCode). PUT is full-replace — omitted optionals cleared not preserved (286.AC7, mirrors backend update-address). Cancel returns to the address book WITHOUT saving (:99-101). Success banner on save (cv-021, copy rename). Reuse buildAddressSchema (m0-11). This increment delivers the FE per-field edit validation surface (286.AC4).
tddTargets: E2E: edit prefilled, change a field, Save -> back to list, success banner, updated value shown
E2E: clearing an optional (addressLine2/county) and saving clears it (full-replace)
E2E: Cancel -> back to list, no change persisted
E2E: invalid edit -> error summary + inline errors, no PUT (286.AC4)

increments.title: Delete confirmation page — two-step soft delete
increments.description: Salvage delete/controller.js + delete/index.njk almost verbatim — already a two-step confirmation interstitial where the soft delete is never reached without the confirmation POST (:29-33). Add the deleteAddress method (forward org header). Cancel -> details page no-op (:71-72); Confirm -> deleteAddress (soft-delete) -> list + deletion banner (:78-86). Changes: field/param renames, copy operator->address, PAGE_TITLE 'Delete operator'->'Delete address' (cv-024/b-delete-confirm). M1 milestone walk-through gate for Sam.
tddTargets: E2E: Delete -> confirmation page; Cancel -> back to details, no delete
E2E: Confirm -> address soft-deleted, back to list with a deletion banner, address gone from the list
M1 walk-through: full CRUD lifecycle add->view->edit->delete on the running stack

increments.title: ?q= server-side search — regex $or over name/townOrCity/postcode + resolved countryCode, org-scoped, excludes deleted
increments.description: Extend list-addresses with ?q= (b-server-search). Salvage the case-insensitive regex $or MECHANISM (OperatorRepository.search @Query :48-58, Pattern.quote, ReDoS-safe) — NOT a Mongo $text index ($text is whole-word/stemmed and fails 186.AC3's partial-word; cv-047). Re-key lead crn->organisationId; KEEP status='ACTIVE' (excludes tombstones, 186.AC6); DELETE the operatorType $in clause + the types param (cv-017); NARROW the $or to name, townOrCity, postcode (186.AC2), dropping addressLine1/2 + county. Keep $options:'i'. Org isolation in the query, not a post-filter (186.AC5). Same single pagination rule (25 config) preserving the active term (186.AC7). Country search (cv-048, FE-resolve placement): the backend accepts the base q PLUS an optional resolved countryCode query term the FE supplies (resolution lives in the FE where the MDM list already is; the backend holds NO reference-data client, keeping the Mongo-only ref-data shape). The DB query ORs raw q over name/townOrCity/postcode AND the resolved code over the stored countryCode field, so 'France' (FE-mapped to 'FR') matches stored 'FR'.
increments.salvageVsRebuild: salvage-modified (mechanism ported, field set narrowed) — OperatorService.list:88-97, OperatorRepository.search:48-58
tddTargets: IT: 'gree' matches 'Green Farm' (partial-word, case-insensitive) — proves regex not $text
IT: raw q matches over name/townOrCity/postcode only, not addressLine1/2/county
IT: a resolved countryCode term ('FR') matches stored 'FR'
IT: search is org-scoped (no cross-org hits) and excludes soft-deleted; results paginate at 25 preserving q + countryCode

increments.title: List search UI — input, country name->code resolution, no-results state, clear-search
increments.description: Keep the server-side ?q= GET form (list/index.njk:27-40); relabel to the ruled searchable set (name/townOrCity/postcode + country). address-book-client.listAddresses forwards q + the FE-resolved countryCode term (cv-048 option A: map any country-NAME hit in q to its alpha-2 code from the MDM list already loaded for the picker, and send it as the countryCode query param). Split the two zero-result render paths (b-server-search): empty-state (m0-13, totalItems===0 AND no q -> 'no addresses yet' + add action) vs no-search-results (totalItems===0 AND q present -> 'no addresses match "<term>"' ECHOING the term back into the search input, already bound value="{{q}}", + a clear-search action). Controller passes a hasSearch=Boolean(q) flag so the view picks the path. Pagination preserves ?q= (+ countryCode) via buildQueryString (186.AC7). M2 milestone walk-through gate.
tddTargets: E2E: partial-word search ('gree') returns matching addresses; case-insensitive
E2E: typing a country name ('France') matches stored 'FR' (FE resolves to code, sends countryCode)
E2E: a no-match search shows 'no addresses match "<term>"' with the term echoed + clear-search, DISTINCT from the empty-state
E2E: clear-search returns to the full list; paging preserves the active term

increments.title: Drop addressLine3 from the notification model + outbox schemaVersion 2->3
increments.description: EUDPA-198 absorbed into 294 as a DUPLICATE. The model already carries the full Standard Address Block (Address.java county:18 + postcode:19; Operator/Transporter telephone+email) — cv-029/cv-030. The ONLY outstanding delta is DROP addressLine3 (Address.java:16, verified present) — a breaking removal. BE: delete the field + its Lombok builder slot (@Builder/@AllArgsConstructor arity changes); sweep grep -rn addressLine3 across BE main+test (builders/DTO/mappers/NotificationSubmittedData/fixtures) BEFORE deleting; verify no consumer reads it first (cv-029). FE: drop the addressLine3 line in formatAddress (notification-view-helper.js:42). Outbox (ji-outbox/cv-031): SCHEMA_VERSION is already '2' (OutboxService.java:25) and ships full party PII; removing addressLine3 changes the emitted shape -> bump to '3' + pin an outbox integration test. The notification Address keeps its city/country field names (198 is scoped to dropping addressLine3 only, not renaming the journey model). NOTE: this is the EUDPA-198 address-block shape trim, NOT the address-book edit-page validation AC (286.AC4 is delivered on the address-book service by m1-02/m1-05); do not read this increment as (re)closing 286.AC4. MODEL-EXTENSION GATE — halt for Sam before rippling the breaking removal.
increments.salvageVsRebuild: rebuild (breaking field removal) — Address.java:16, notification-view-helper.js:42, OutboxService.java:25
tddTargets: Unit/IT: Address no longer has addressLine3; all builders/mappers compile against the reduced arity
Outbox IT: the emitted address block has no addressLine3 and schemaVersion == '3'
FE: formatAddress renders name/addressLine1/addressLine2/city/county/postcode/country/telephone/email (no addressLine3), county/telephone/email still present (cv-030)

increments.title: New hydrating resolve() client method — values + deleted flag
increments.description: OperatorsApiClient.classify() (:43-71) is deliberately values-free (reads only status off OperatorStatusProbe, class Javadoc :13-17). Resolution needs the opposite. Add a NEW method resolve(addressId) -> ResolvedAddress that GETs the by-id address from the Address Book API org-path /organisation/{orgId}/addresses/{id} (INCLUDING soft-deleted, flagged; camelCase body cv-001; forwarding the caller's session org as trusted identity Trade-Imports-Organisation-Id, cv-040/cv-042). Returns a values-carrying type PLUS the deleted flag (one call serves both hydration and the deleted signal — no second classify round-trip). Maps UNAVAILABLE/404 to the same distinct outcomes as classify (ACTIVE/DELETED/NOT_FOUND/UNAVAILABLE) so the read-check/guard branching barely changes. Do NOT widen classify to leak values — add resolve alongside; classify stays for any pure existence need.
tddTargets: Unit (nock/wiremock at the HTTP boundary): resolve() of a live address returns its values + deleted=false; forwards the org header to the org-path
Unit: resolve() of a soft-deleted address returns values + deleted=true (by-id surfaces the tombstone)
Unit: 404 -> NOT_FOUND, connection failure -> UNAVAILABLE (distinct outcomes)
Unit: classify() is unchanged and still values-free

increments.title: Reference|inline party sum-type + narrow parties 7->4 + resolve-on-read hydrator
increments.description: Reshape the party model to a reference|inline sum-type (294.AC5): a referenceable role holds an addressId reference (values resolved on read, not the persisted source of truth); place-of-origin (D24) + consignment (D26) stay inline (no addressId, never resolved). Discriminator = StringUtils.isNotBlank(operatorId) (already used at :205-208); single-class-with-optional-addressId layout is the dev's call (cv-012). Narrow operatorParties (:184-197) from 7 to the 4 referenceable roles — consignor/consignee/importer/destination — dropping placeOfOrigin (:186), consignment (:191), transporter (:192-195, cv-013). This one method feeds both the read check (:154) and the submit guard (:259). Repurpose applyOperatorExistenceCheck (:148-182) from classify-only to a hydrator: for each referenceable party with an addressId, call resolve() (m3-02), populate values onto NotificationResponse (latest details) WITHOUT writing back to Mongo (no fan-out into drafts, 294.AC4); keep the deleted signal (resolved-but-soft-deleted -> deletedOperatorFields, cv-016); keep degrade-open on UNAVAILABLE (:161-166); SUBMITTED is never resolved (status guard :150-153 stays). N+1: dedupe resolution once per distinct addressId per request (extend the existing .distinct() at :159 to the list path findAll :213-221); no cross-request cache (measure first).
tddTargets: IT: a Draft/Amend read reflects the LATEST edited address values via resolve-on-read, no trader action (replaces the v1 staleness pin which now asserts the wrong behaviour)
IT: resolution does NOT write values back to Mongo (no fan-out)
IT: only the 4 referenceable roles resolve; placeOfOrigin/consignment/transporter never resolve
IT: a resolve outage (UNAVAILABLE) degrades open — the page renders, no blanking
IT: list-view resolution dedupes per distinct addressId per request (N+1 mitigation)

increments.title: Repoint the 4 select pages to the Address Book API; remove inline/deferred selects; store a reference
increments.description: ji-select-pages (294.AC1/AC2). The four keep-pages addresses/{consignors,consignees,importers,destinations}/select: repoint operatorsClient.listOperators at the Address Book API org-path /organisation/{orgId}/addresses (was /operators :167), camelCase response (rewrite FIELD_MAP/fromApiOperator :10-71, page/pageSize/totalItems/totalPages reads); DROP the type filter (OPERATOR_TYPE/operatorType :19,:32 + :172-174) — the org's WHOLE book is offered (untyped, cv-017); STORE a reference not a copy — replace toNotificationOperator(selected) (:83, copies name/address/telephone/email) with storing JUST the addressId into the party (BE resolves on read via the hydrator, m3-03, against the list-addresses API, m0-06); org-scope session-sourced (request.auth.credentials.organisationId into the PATH never the query string, D23/cv-010; forwarded org authorised vs path orgId, cv-040/cv-042). The list endpoint already excludes soft-deleted (clean select list); a stale reference is caught by the review gate (M4). REMOVE addresses/place-of-origin/select + addresses/consignment/contact/select (inline now, D24/D26, cv-014 inverts v1 j-002/j-004) and transporters/select (cv-013) — routes/controllers/views/tests go; inline entry pages stay as the data source. Select-by-id (cv-020) is already true (:64-66). DEPENDS on m3-03 (the BE resolve-on-read hydrator) and m0-06 (the list-addresses API these select pages call) — storing only the addressId is unsafe to ship before the hydrator that resolves it, else a Draft/Amend read would show blank party details.
tddTargets: E2E: a select page shows the org's real address book (org-wide, deleted excluded), select stores the addressId reference (no value copy in session)
Component ×4: no type param sent; call goes to the org-path with the org header
Tests: place-of-origin/consignment/transporter select routes are gone; inline entry pages remain

increments.title: Terminology sweep operator -> address / consignment party (journey side)
increments.description: cv-015 (D13): retire 'operator' journey-side. Rename deletedOperatorFields/unresolvedOperatorFields (BE NotificationResponse:44-45; FE operator-party-view-keys.js), the toNotificationOperator/fromApiOperator client names, OPERATOR_SUBMISSION_ERROR copy (declaration/controller.js:9), and the review-page messages ('this operator has been deleted', operator-party-view-keys.js:47-49) to 'address'/'consignment party'. Trim OPERATOR_PARTY_VIEW_KEYS to the four referenceable roles (placeOfOrigin/consignment/transporter keys are dead once the BE stops emitting them, m3-03). A wide rename riding alongside the functional change; re-baseline the notification-view + declaration controller tests. M3 milestone walk-through gate for Sam.
tddTargets: Re-baselined notification-view + declaration tests assert the four-role key set and 'address'/'consignment party' copy
Grep: no 'operator' vocabulary remains on the referenceable-party surfaces
M3 walk-through: select an address into a party, edit that address, confirm the Draft reflects the latest values (resolve-on-read)

increments.title: FE review-page deleted-address gate — summary + inline + block submit + replace clears
increments.description: ji-deleted-gate 293.AC1/AC3 — salvage in place. The review page already renders detection: buildOperatorErrors (notification-view/controller.js:30, operator-party-view-keys.js:78-87) turns deletedOperatorFields into a summary message ('select a replacement') + a per-role inline entry with an anchor into the relevant summary card (:56-66,:9-45), rendered as an error summary (:37-40) — AC1 (summary + inline per affected role) and AC3 (inline message links to the relevant part) already built. Reshape: keys already trimmed to four roles (m3-05); BLOCK SUBMIT while any deleted-address error stands (AC1) — gate the 'Continue to declaration' affordance on operatorErrors having no deleted entries (the authoritative block is BE m4-02, but the FE must not send the user into submit with a known-bad reference); choosing a replacement clears it (AC3) — re-selecting a valid address on the role's select page (m3-04) overwrites the addressId, the next review load resolves clean, buildOperatorErrors returns empty (falls out of resolve-on-read, no new mechanism). Applies to Draft AND Amend (cv-027).
tddTargets: E2E: a Draft/Amend referencing a since-deleted address shows a 'select a replacement' summary + per-role inline message linking to that part; Continue-to-declaration is blocked
E2E: selecting a valid replacement clears the error and allows submit
Applies to both Draft and Amend

increments.title: BE authoritative submit reject on a deleted-address reference
increments.description: ji-deleted-gate 293.AC2 — salvage guardOperatorExistenceForSubmit (NotificationService.java:258-304), already fail-closed and keyed per party field (OperatorValidationException :303), rejecting a direct-API submit regardless of the frontend, naming each affected role, leaving status unchanged (the throw precedes writeWithOutbox — :235 before :237). Reshape: move from classify() (:268) to resolve() (m3-02) — a soft-deleted address resolves flagged-deleted -> DELETED leg (:281-284, the 293.AC2 leg); a clean submit passes and proceeds. The guard walks operatorParties, narrowed to four (m3-03), so placeOfOrigin/consignment/transporter no longer gate. Identity: the required-identity check stays (no silent bypass) but keys on the forwarded session ORG (cv-010/cv-040); resolve() forwards it as the Trade-Imports-Organisation-Id-scoped call. FE surfacing already exists (declaration/controller.js maps 400->submission-error, 502->verification-unavailable) — keep, copy retired in m3-05.
tddTargets: IT: a direct-API submit referencing a soft-deleted address -> 400 naming each affected role, status unchanged
IT: a clean submit (all references live) passes the guard
IT: NOT_FOUND -> per-field 400; UNAVAILABLE -> 502 (fail-closed on submit)
IT: only the four referenceable roles gate; an inline place-of-origin never triggers a deleted-address reject

increments.title: Freeze-on-submit snapshot — move capture amend-start->submit, retain addressId, SUBMITTED reads frozen
increments.description: ji-freeze-submit — reuse the existing NotificationContentSnapshot machinery (NotificationContentSnapshot.java, NotificationContentSnapshotMapper deep-clone, Notification.submittedBaseline :24-25). Change WHEN/WHAT it captures. AC1: DELETE the amend-start capture (amendNotification :317) — amend must not freeze, it re-resolves live (AC5); capture at SUBMIT in the SAME transaction as DRAFT/AMEND->SUBMITTED — today writeWithOutbox NULLS the baseline on AMEND->SUBMITTED (:368-371); replace that null with a capture of the RESOLVED details (run capture AFTER m3-03 resolution has populated party values; NotificationContentSnapshot.from(notification) :31-33 deep-clones whatever values are on the parties). AC2: retain the addressId alongside (additive — operatorId is a mapped field on the snapshot party, ensure it is not stripped). AC3: SUBMITTED reads the frozen snapshot — prefer service-projection (keep submittedBaseline @JsonIgnore internal, project the baseline into the NotificationResponse party fields for SUBMITTED, mirroring how the read check projects; extend the status fork at :150-153). AC5: amend re-resolves live (correct by construction once capture is removed from amendNotification). AC6: cancel-amend restores the submit-time snapshot (cancelAmendNotification :328-349 restores from submittedBaseline; verify amend leaves the submit-time baseline intact). AC4: post-submit edit/delete of the referenced address does not change the submitted view and must not error on a dangling id (SUBMITTED never resolves).
tddTargets: IT (AC1/AC3): a clean submit captures the resolved snapshot in the submit transaction and transitions SUBMITTED; the SUBMITTED read returns frozen values
IT (AC2): the frozen party carries both the frozen values AND the addressId
IT (AC4): editing/deleting the referenced address after submit does not change the SUBMITTED view and does not error on a dangling id
IT (AC5): amend re-resolves live and behaves as Draft
IT (AC6): cancel-amend restores the submit-time snapshot

increments.title: Validate-then-freeze ordering + submit-flow integration test (295 lands second)
increments.description: EUDPA-293 and EUDPA-295 mutate the SAME submit transaction; EUDPA-295 lands SECOND, so this increment owns the ordering guarantee (ji-freeze-submit/ji-deleted-gate sequencing hazard). The hard invariant: the deleted-address gate (m4-02, guardOperatorExistenceForSubmit at :235) runs BEFORE the snapshot capture (m4-03, in writeWithOutbox at :237) — otherwise a soft-deleted address is frozen into the legal record. In the current structure the order is naturally preserved (guard :235 then writeWithOutbox :237); this increment PINS it so neither ticket's future edits can reorder it. Pin with a submit-flow integration test asserting BOTH halves: a submit referencing a soft-deleted address is REJECTED (400, role named) AND no snapshot is written (baseline unchanged, no SUBMITTED transition, no outbox event) — the rejection alone does not prove the freeze did not run. Companion: a clean submit captures the resolved snapshot AND transitions SUBMITTED.
increments.salvageVsRebuild: new test + ordering assertion over m4-02/m4-03 — NotificationService.submitNotification:223,235,237
tddTargets: Submit-flow IT: deleted-ref submit -> 400 (role named) AND baseline null/unchanged, no SUBMITTED, no outbox event
Submit-flow IT (companion): clean submit -> resolved snapshot captured + SUBMITTED transition + outbox event
Order assertion: the deleted-gate executes before snapshot capture in the submit transaction

increments.title: EUDPA-286.AC8 — edits reach drafts, never submitted notifications (follow-on gated behind 295)
increments.description: The AC8 follow-on that could not be closed in M1: an address edit reaches Draft/Amend notifications (via resolve-on-read, m3-03) but NEVER a submitted notification (which reads the frozen snapshot, m4-03). Untestable until 294 (resolve) + 295 (freeze) exist — blockingGraph acBlockedBy AC8:[EUDPA-295]. This increment verifies and pins the end-to-end behaviour across the two mechanisms. Hard gate before go-live (EUDPA-295) — M4 milestone walk-through gate for Sam.
increments.salvageVsRebuild: verification/pinning over m3-03 + m4-03 — no new production surface
tddTargets: E2E/IT: edit an address referenced by a Draft -> the Draft shows the new values; edit an address referenced by a SUBMITTED notification -> the submitted view is unchanged (frozen)
E2E/IT: the same holds for an Amend (re-resolves live) vs its pre-amend submitted snapshot
M4 walk-through: the full submit gate + freeze lifecycle on the running stack
